{{< include /_macros.qmd >}}

# The Language Modelling Problem {#sec-language-modelling}

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## In this chapter
<!-- 3-5 sentences: what is a language model, and why is "predict the next
     token" a serious learning objective rather than a party trick? -->
:::

**Learning objectives.** After this chapter you should be able to:

<!-- TODO -->
- [ ]
- [ ]
- [ ]

## Language as a probability distribution

<!-- Text as samples from an unknown distribution over sequences. The chain
     rule / autoregressive factorization p(x) = prod_t p(x_t | x_{<t}). -->

## The pretraining objective

<!-- Maximum likelihood = next-token prediction = cross-entropy. Teacher
     forcing. Why this single objective is enough to learn so much. -->

## Measuring a model

<!-- Cross-entropy, perplexity, bits-per-byte. The compression connection
     (Shannon). What a "good" number even means. -->

## Generation

<!-- From a distribution to text: sampling, temperature, greedy/argmax.
     Kept brief — decoding is not the focus of a pretraining book. -->

## Exercises {.unnumbered}

<!-- TODO -->

## Further reading {.unnumbered}

<!-- e.g. Shannon [@shannon1948] -->
